channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Slang Context-based Inference Enhancement via Greedy Search-Guided Chain-of-Thought Prompting
item.description: arXiv:2603.13230v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Slang interpretation has been a challenging downstream task for Large Language Models (LLMs) as the expressions are inherently embedded in contextual, cultural, and linguistic frameworks. In the absence of domain-specific training data, it is difficul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, experiment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Steering at the Source: Style Modulation Heads for Robust Persona Control
item.description: arXiv:2603.13249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering offers a computationally efficient mechanism for controlling Large Language Models (LLMs) without fine-tuning. While effectively controlling target traits (e.g., persona), coherency degradation remains a major obstacle to safety an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, large language model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Transformers Reject Wrong Answers: Rotational Dynamics of Factual Constraint Processing
item.description: arXiv:2603.13259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a language model is fed a wrong answer, what happens inside the network? Current understanding treats truthfulness as a static property of individual-layer representations-a direction to be probed, a feature to be extracted. Less is known about t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, vector, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Design and evaluation of an agentic workflow for crisis-related synthetic tweet datasets
item.description: arXiv:2603.13625v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Twitter (now X) has become an important source of social media data for situational awareness during crises. Crisis informatics research has widely used tweets from Twitter to develop and evaluate artificial intelligence (AI) systems for various crisi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, research, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Widespread Gender and Pronoun Bias in Moral Judgments Across LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.13636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to assess moral or ethical statements, yet their judgments may reflect social and linguistic biases. This work presents a controlled, sentence-level study of how grammatical person, number, and gender...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Privacy Preserving Topic-wise Sentiment Analysis of the Iran Israel USA Conflict Using Federated Transformer Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.13655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent escalation of the Iran Israel USA conflict in 2026 has triggered widespread global discussions across social media platforms. As people increasingly use these platforms for expressing opinions, analyzing public sentiment from these discussi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, platform, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Human Attribution of Causality to AI Across Agency, Misuse, and Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse outcome, the problem of cause selection, is a crit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, arxiv, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Think First, Diffuse Fast: Improving Diffusion Language Model Reasoning via Autoregressive Plan Conditioning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text via iterative denoising but consistently underperform on multi-step reasoning. We hypothesize this gap stems from a coordination problem: AR models build coherence token-by-token, while diffusion m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, context, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Translational Gaps in Graph Transformers for Longitudinal EHR Prediction: A Critical Appraisal of GT-BEHRT
item.description: arXiv:2603.13231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformer-based models have improved predictive modeling on longitudinal electronic health records through large-scale self-supervised pretraining. However, most EHR transformer architectures treat each clinical encounter as an unordered collection ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, transformer, paper, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Axis Trust Modeling for Interpretable Account Hijacking Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.13246v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a Hadith-inspired multi-axis trust modeling framework, motivated by a structurally analogous problem in classical Hadith scholarship: assessing the trustworthiness of information sources using interpretable, multidimensional criter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, context, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory
item.description: arXiv:2603.13258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While "Intent-oriented programming" (or "Vibe Coding") redefines software engineering, existing code agents remain tethered to static code snapshots. Consequently, they struggle to model the critical information embedded in the temporal evolution of p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, memory, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Attention: True Adaptive World Models via Spherical Kernel Operator
item.description: arXiv:2603.13263v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The pursuit of world model based artificial intelligence has predominantly relied on projecting high-dimensional observations into parameterized latent spaces, wherein transition dynamics are subsequently learned. However, this conventional paradigm i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, product, framework, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Automating Document Intelligence in Statutory City Planning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: UK planning authorities face a legislative conflict between the Planning Act, which mandates public access to application documents, and the Data Protection Act, which requires protection of personal information. This situation creates a manually inte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, framework, study, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models struggle with "zero-day" attacks due to extreme clas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, paper, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
